release_notes: Figure now walks ON TOP of the Ready button during the random cross-canvas movement, instead of disappearing whenever it crossed the button. Previously AnimationPlayer._tick drew the button mask on top of the figure every single frame — that's what produced the peek-out effect at the start, but it also meant any walk that crossed the button area made the figure vanish mid-stride. Moved the _draw_button_mask call out of _tick and into _emerge and _hide_back, where it's the LAST thing drawn in each of those phases. Net result: the peek-out and peek-back-in effects still work exactly as before (mask draws over the figure during emerge/hide_back so the figure appears to rise/sink behind the button), but during walks and activity phases there's no mask at all — the overlay canvas is transparent everywhere except where the figure is drawn, so the real Ready button beneath the overlay shows through and the figure walks on top of it.
